title: phragmanthera fulva
intro: Phragmanthera fulva, also known as the yellow mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of trees in tropical regions. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Phragmanthera fulva can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, and is also sometimes used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
